questions.question: Which is listed as a reason for studying concepts of programming languages?

choices.D: Removal of the need for documentation

questions.question: Which reason for studying programming languages relates to making better choices when selecting a language for a project?

choices.B: Improved background for choosing appropriate languages
choices.C: Better understanding of significance of implementation

questions.question: Which programming domain is associated with large numbers of floating point computations and use of arrays?
Which language is listed under business applications?
Which language is listed under artificial intelligence and manipulates symbols rather than numbers?
Why is efficiency particularly important in systems programming?

choices.A: Because it uses floating point operations

questions.question: Which evaluation criterion refers to the ease with which programs can be read and understood?
Which evaluation criterion is defined as conformance to specifications?
Which evaluation criterion includes training programmers, writing programs, executing programs, and maintaining programs?
Under readability, which item is listed as part of overall simplicity?
Which readability factor states that a relatively small set of primitive constructs can be combined in a relatively small number of ways?
According to the lesson on orthogonality, every possible combination should be what?
Which syntax consideration for readability involves flexible composition?
Which writability factor is described as the ability to define and use complex structures or operations in ways that allow details to be ignored?
Expressivity in writability refers to what?

choices.D: A set of relatively convenient ways of specifying operations

questions.question: Which reliability factor involves testing for type errors?
What is aliasing in the context of reliability?

choices.A: Using multiple names for the same function
choices.B: Presence of two or more distinct referencing methods for the same memory location
choices.D: Creating shortcuts for long variable names

questions.question: Exception handling in reliability involves what?

choices.A: Preventing all errors at compile time
choices.C: Intercepting run-time errors and taking corrective measures

questions.question: How does reliability relate to readability and writability according to the lesson?

choices.A: A language that doesn't support natural ways of expressing algorithms will have reduced reliability
choices.D: Writability must be sacrificed for reliability

questions.question: Portability is defined as what?

choices.A: The ease of learning new languages
choices.B: The completeness of the official definition
choices.C: The ability to define complex structures
choices.D: The ease with which programs can be moved from one implementation to another

questions.question: Generality as an evaluation criterion refers to what?

choices.A: The number of users of the language
choices.B: The applicability to a wide range of applications

questions.question: Well-definedness refers to what aspect of a programming language?

choices.C: The completeness and precision of the language's official definition

questions.question: Which is listed as an influence on language design?
Program design methodologies as an influence on language design led to what?

choices.B: New programming paradigms and new programming languages

questions.question: Which well-known computer architecture influenced the development of imperative languages?
In the von Neumann architecture, where are data and programs stored?
Which characteristic of von Neumann architecture is modeled by variables in imperative languages?
Assignment statements in imperative languages model what aspect of von Neumann architecture?

choices.A: Piping of data from memory to CPU

questions.question: In the fetch-execute cycle, what happens after fetching the instruction pointed by the counter?
What is the von Neumann bottleneck?

choices.B: The connection speed between memory and processor that limits computer speed
choices.C: The number of registers in the CPU
choices.D: The clock speed of the processor

questions.question: During the 1950s and early 1960s programming methodology period, what was the primary concern?
What became important in the late 1960s programming methodology era?

choices.A: People efficiency, readability, and better control structures

questions.question: Structured programming and top-down design emerged in which era?
The shift from process-oriented to data-oriented programming occurred in which era?
Object-oriented programming emerged in which decade?
What three key features characterize object-oriented programming?
Which language category has variables, assignment statements, and iteration as central features?
In functional languages, the main means of computation is by doing what?
Which language category is described as rule-based with rules specified in no particular order?
Which languages are listed as examples of functional programming languages?
Which language is listed as an example of a logic language?
The reliability vs. cost of execution trade-off is exemplified by which language feature?
Which trade-off example states that APL provides many powerful operators but at the cost of poor readability?
C++ pointers are cited as an example of which trade-off?
In compilation, which phase converts characters in the source program into lexical units?
Which compilation phase transforms lexical units into parse trees?
What is the primary characteristic of compilation in terms of speed?
Where are compiled programs typically used?
What is a load module?

choices.A: The user and system code together (executable image)

questions.question: What is linking and loading?

choices.A: Translating source code to machine code
choices.B: The process of collecting system program units and linking them to a user program

questions.question: Which statement about pure interpretation is listed in Lesson 1?

choices.A: It always requires compilation to machine code
choices.B: It is faster than compiled programs
choices.D: It replaces the need for an interpreter

questions.question: How much slower are interpreted programs compared to compiled programs according to the lesson?
What advantage does pure interpretation offer?

choices.A: Easier implementation of programs with run-time errors easily displayed

questions.question: Which scripting languages brought a comeback to pure interpretation?
Hybrid implementation systems are a compromise between what?
What intermediate form does Java use in its hybrid implementation?
What does JVM stand for?
What are JIT systems?

choices.C: Systems that initially translate to intermediate language then compile to machine code when called

questions.question: How are JIT systems characterized in the lesson?
Which languages are implemented with JIT systems?
What does a preprocessor do according to the lesson?

choices.A: Optimizes machine code at run time
choices.D: Expands embedded preprocessor macros before compilation

questions.question: Which is a well-known example of a preprocessor?
What does the C preprocessor expand?
What is a programming environment?

choices.C: A collection of tools used in software development

questions.question: Which programming environment is described as an older operating system and tool collection?
What is Microsoft Visual Studio.NET described as in the lesson?
NetBeans is related to Visual Studio.NET except it's for applications in which language?
